# Source Index

## accc_photobookshop_2026

**Title:** ACCC — PhotobookShop pays penalties for influencer reviews (2026)

**Tier:** Regulator / primary

**URL:** https://www.accc.gov.au/media-release/photobookshop-pays-penalties-for-influencer-reviews

**Notes:** PhotobookShop paid AU$39,600 in penalties after allegedly instructing influencers not to disclose gifted products on 107 occasions and editing negative review comments out of a reposted influencer review.

## amazon_fake_review_brokers

**Title:** Amazon continues legal actions to protect customers from fake reviews

**Tier:** Company enforcement / primary

**URL:** https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-continues-legal-actions-to-protect-customers-from-fake-reviews

**Notes:** Amazon reported legal actions against fake review brokers and said nearly 350,000 people using broker websites were no longer incentivized to write fake/misleading Amazon reviews.

## amazon_review_groups

**Title:** Amazon targets fake review fraudsters on social media

**Tier:** Company enforcement / primary

**URL:** https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-targets-fake-review-fraudsters-on-social-media

**Notes:** Amazon filed legal action against administrators of more than 10,000 Facebook groups allegedly orchestrating fake reviews in exchange for money/free products.

## ap_ai_scams_ads

**Title:** AP — AI is a gold mine for spammers and scammers, but Google is using it as a tool to fight back

**Tier:** News / reputable

**URL:** https://apnews.com/article/06d9ef869958555884989e8ec25974be

**Notes:** AP reports generative AI is being used to create deceptive ads at scale; FBI logged over 22,000 AI-related scam complaints in 2025 totaling $893M in losses.

## apple_app_store_fraud_2025

**Title:** Apple App Store fraud analysis 2025

**Tier:** Platform transparency / primary

**URL:** https://www.apple.com/au/newsroom/2025/05/the-app-store-prevented-more-than-9-billion-usd-in-fraudulent-transactions/

**Notes:** Apple said it stopped over US$2B in potentially fraudulent transactions in 2024, removed 143M+ fraudulent ratings/reviews, 7,400+ potentially fraudulent apps from charts, and nearly 9,500 deceptive apps from search.

## appsflyer_field_guide

**Title:** AppsFlyer mobile ad fraud field guide

**Tier:** Industry report

**URL:** https://www.appsflyer.com/resources/reports/apac-mobile-ad-fraud/

**Notes:** Defines install hijacking, click flooding and fake installs as mobile-ad-fraud categories; explains attribution hijacking steals credit for installs from other sources.

## appsflyer_validation_rules

**Title:** AppsFlyer validation rules to prevent fraud

**Tier:** Industry documentation / primary

**URL:** https://support.appsflyer.com/hc/en-us/articles/115004703926-Implement-validation-rules-to-prevent-fraud

**Notes:** Rules can block installs and in-app event attributions for hijacked installs, bots, emulators, device farms and fake installs/events.

## bi_clipping_authenticity

**Title:** Business Insider — Clipping makes me wonder what is real and what is not

**Tier:** News / business reporting

**URL:** https://www.businessinsider.com/clipping-social-media-tiktok-viral-band-geese-advertising-authentic-fake-2026-4

**Notes:** Frames clipping as paid/incentivized short-form snippets that blur organic content and marketing.

## bi_elite_clippers

**Title:** Business Insider — Elite clippers are earning big paychecks

**Tier:** News / business reporting

**URL:** https://www.businessinsider.com/clipping-creators-arrived-discord-money-earning-big-2026-3

**Notes:** Reports retainers/performance payouts, $1–$4 per 1,000 views, clipper marketplaces and Discord/Whop/Genni/Vyro ecosystems.

## bloomberg_arr

**Title:** Bloomberg — What is ARR? Behind least-trusted metric of AI era

**Tier:** News / business reporting

**URL:** https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-07/what-is-arr-behind-the-least-trusted-metric-of-the-ai-era?srnd=phx-ai

**Notes:** Uses Cluely as a case and frames ARR as increasingly distrusted in the AI-startup context.

## deezer_ai_music_2026

**Title:** Deezer — AI-generated tracks represent 44% of new uploaded music

**Tier:** Platform transparency / primary

**URL:** https://newsroom-deezer.com/2026/04/ai-generated-tracks-represent-44-of-new-uploaded-music/

**Notes:** Deezer says it receives almost 75,000 AI-generated tracks per day, about 44% of daily uploads; 85% of streams on fully AI-generated tracks were detected as fraudulent and demonetized in 2025.

## doj_michael_smith

**Title:** DOJ — Michael Smith AI-assisted music streaming fraud guilty plea

**Tier:** Law enforcement / primary

**URL:** https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/pr/north-carolina-man-pleads-guilty-music-streaming-fraud-aided-artificial-intelligence-0

**Notes:** DOJ says Smith created hundreds of thousands of AI-generated songs and used bots to stream them billions of times to mimic genuine listening and steal royalty payments.

## ftc_fake_reviews_rule

**Title:** FTC final rule banning fake reviews and testimonials (2024)

**Tier:** Regulator / primary

**URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/08/federal-trade-commission-announces-final-rule-banning-fake-reviews-testimonials

**Notes:** Prohibits fake/false reviews and testimonials, buying reviews conditioned on sentiment, review suppression, insider reviews without disclosure, and misuse of fake social-media indicators.

## ftc_icpen_dark_patterns

**Title:** FTC/ICPEN/GPEN dark patterns sweep results

**Tier:** Regulator / primary

**URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/07/ftc-icpen-gpen-announce-results-review-use-dark-patterns-affecting-subscription-services-privacy

**Notes:** Review of 642 subscription websites/apps found nearly 76% used at least one possible dark pattern and nearly 67% used multiple possible dark patterns.

## ftc_reviews_qna

**Title:** FTC Consumer Reviews and Testimonials Rule Q&A

**Tier:** Regulator / primary

**URL:** https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/consumer-reviews-testimonials-rule-questions-answers

**Notes:** Clarifies fake social media indicators and business responsibility around reviews/testimonials.

## github_acceptable_use

**Title:** GitHub Acceptable Use Policies

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/acceptable-use-policies/github-acceptable-use-policies

**Notes:** Bans spam and inauthentic activity including fake accounts, automated inauthentic activity, rank abuse such as automated starring/following, and secondary markets for inauthentic activity.

## google_ads_safety_2025

**Title:** Google 2025 Ads Safety Report

**Tier:** Platform transparency / primary

**URL:** https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/2025-ads-safety-report/

**Notes:** Google said Gemini-powered tools stopped over 99% of policy-violating ads before they ran in 2025; blocked/removed over 8.3B ads and suspended 24.9M accounts, including 602M scam ads and 4M scam-associated accounts.

## google_maps_fake_engagement

**Title:** Google Business Profile / Maps prohibited content — fake engagement

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://support.google.com/business/answer/2622994?hl=en

**Notes:** Google Maps contributions must reflect genuine experience; fake engagement includes incentivized reviews, multiple accounts to manipulate rating, and emulator/device-tampering methods.

## google_play_ratings_reviews

**Title:** Google Play policy — User Ratings, Reviews, and Installs

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/9898684?hl=en

**Notes:** Developers must not manipulate app placement by inflating ratings, reviews, or installs through fraudulent or incentivized means.

## google_search_45_percent

**Title:** Google Search update results — 45% less low-quality unoriginal content

**Tier:** Platform blog / primary

**URL:** https://blog.google/products/search/google-search-update-march-2024/

**Notes:** Google said its March 2024 rollout produced 45% less low-quality, unoriginal content in Search results.

## google_search_spam_2024

**Title:** Google Search March 2024 update / spam policies

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2024/03/core-update-spam-policies

**Notes:** Targets scaled content abuse, site reputation abuse, and expired domain abuse.

## linkedin_authentic_2026

**Title:** LinkedIn — Supporting Authentic Content and Conversations (2026)

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://news.linkedin.com/2026/authentic-content-and-conversations

**Notes:** LinkedIn says it limits reach of inauthentic activity, including engagement pods and automated comments, and expanded profile verification to more than 100 million members.

## meta_original_creators_2026

**Title:** Meta — Rewarding Original Creators on Facebook (2026)

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/rewarding-original-creators-on-facebook/

**Notes:** Facebook is deprioritizing unoriginal content, clarifying original-content guidelines, and says it removed more than 20 million accounts impersonating large creators in 2025.

## oecd_dark_patterns

**Title:** OECD — Dark commercial patterns

**Tier:** Policy / intergovernmental

**URL:** https://www.oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/dark-commercial-patterns.html

**Notes:** Dark commercial patterns steer, deceive, coerce, or manipulate consumers into decisions not in their best interest; exploit biases to extract money, data or attention.

## product_hunt_guidelines

**Title:** Product Hunt Community Guidelines

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://help.producthunt.com/en/articles/3615694-community-guidelines

**Notes:** Prohibits mass messaging users, asking for upvotes, bots, incentivized upvotes, and other artificial activity.

## product_hunt_points

**Title:** Product Hunt — What are points?

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://help.producthunt.com/en/articles/10275873-what-are-points

**Notes:** Rankings use points calculated from genuine engagement including upvotes and other signals such as comments, not raw upvote count alone.

## rogers_righetti_2025

**Title:** Rogers & Righetti (2025) — Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention

**Tier:** Academic / open-access

**URL:** https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784

**Notes:** Defines manufactured attention as artificial audience fabrication through coordinated behaviour; surfaces advertising networks, gambling/cyber-scam promotion, hijacked public groups, media groups, and political/activist coordination.

## springer_recommenders_2026

**Title:** A comprehensive survey on multi-behavior recommender systems (2026)

**Tier:** Academic / open-access

**URL:** https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s41060-025-01019-z

**Notes:** Shows modern recommender systems learn from many interaction types such as clicks, carts, purchases and ratings; useful for explaining why proxy-signal manipulation is powerful.

## techcrunch_cluely

**Title:** TechCrunch — Cluely CEO admits publicly lying about revenue numbers

**Tier:** News / business reporting

**URL:** https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/05/cluely-ceo-roy-lee-admits-to-publicly-lying-about-revenue-numbers-last-year/

**Notes:** Reports Cluely CEO Roy Lee admitted the $7M ARR figure he gave TechCrunch in 2025 was false.

**Tier:** News / business reporting

**URL:** https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/04/embattled-startup-delve-has-parted-ways-with-y-combinator/

**Notes:** Reported allegations, company denials, and YC split. Must be framed as contested/reported allegations, not settled fact.

## techcrunch_psyop

**Title:** TechCrunch — Everything we like is a psyop

**Tier:** News / analysis

**URL:** https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/16/everything-we-like-is-a-psyop/

**Notes:** Includes quoted explanation of posting enough volume across enough accounts to simulate a song trending; describes Phia creator-farm strategy and volume of creator videos.

**URL:** https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911267/ai-influencers-coachella

**Notes:** Reports AI-generated influencers around Coachella and disclosure concerns.

## tiktok_integrity

**Title:** TikTok Community Guidelines — Integrity and Authenticity

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://www.tiktok.com/community-guidelines/en/integrity-authenticity/

**Notes:** Bans fake engagement, trade/marketing of engagement-inflation services, covert influence operations, bulk account operation, manipulation of engagement signals, fake personas and spam.

## trustpilot_trust_report_2025

**Title:** Trustpilot Trust Report 2025

**Tier:** Platform transparency / primary

**URL:** https://corporate.trustpilot.com/trust/trust-report-2025

**Notes:** Trustpilot reported 7.4% of submitted reviews detected and removed as fake in 2024; reported 3.7 million fabricated reviews removed automatically and 19k warnings issued.

## vanityfair_ai_clones

**Title:** Vanity Fair — Influencers AI clones

**Tier:** News / cultural reporting

**URL:** https://www.vanityfair.com/news/story/influencers-ai-clones

**Notes:** Discusses influencer AI clones/digital twins, likeness control, consent, contract rights, fan trust and audience disclosure.

## wired_geese

**Title:** WIRED — The Fanfare Around the Band Geese Actually Was a Psyop

**Tier:** News / cultural reporting

**URL:** https://www.wired.com/story/geese-chaotic-good-marketing-industry-plant/

**Notes:** Reports on Chaotic Good Projects, Geese, networks of social pages, TikTok recommendation-seeding, burner accounts/comments, and the phrase “trend simulation”.

## x_authenticity

**Title:** X Authenticity / platform manipulation policy

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://help.x.com/ro/rules-and-policies/platform-manipulation

**Notes:** Bans inauthentic accounts, fake personas, engagement exchanges, compensated metric inflation, follow churn and automated aggressive engagement.

## youtube_fake_engagement

**Title:** YouTube Fake Engagement Policy

**Tier:** Platform policy / primary

**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/3399767?hl=en-GB

**Notes:** YouTube does not allow artificial increases in views, likes, comments, or other metrics; actions by hired promoters can affect the channel.
